message:
test(cli-doctor): pin top-level auto-fix fields on derivative-cleanup fix

\`doctor_fix_prunes_safe_derivative_cleanup_candidates\` previously
asserted the nested \`cleanup_apply\` block of the doctor JSON
payload (\`requested: true\`, \`applied: true\`, etc.) but left
the TOP-LEVEL doctor fields un-pinned. Operators and dashboards
read the top-level \`auto_fix_applied\`, \`auto_fix_actions\`,
and \`issues_fixed\` counters first, and the per-check
\`derivative_cleanup\` block is what \`/admin\` surfaces to
humans. A regression that silently dropped the cleanup from the
top-level counters would leave the nested block intact and this
test would still pass.

Tighten the assertions:

- \`payload[\"auto_fix_applied\"]\` is \`true\` — pins the header
  flag operators read first.
- \`payload[\"auto_fix_actions\"]\` contains a string that
  includes \`\"Pruned 2 derivative cleanup artifact(s)\"\` — pins
  the exact human-readable action string so the counters and
  pluralization don't drift.
- \`payload[\"issues_fixed\"]\` is \`>= 1\` — the cleanup must
  count as at least one fixed issue; using \`>=\` keeps the test
  stable if adjacent fixes are also applied in this same run.
- \`payload[\"checks\"][derivative_cleanup]\` has
  \`status == \"pass\"\`, \`fix_available == true\`,
  \`fix_applied == true\` — pins the per-check block exactly
  so a regression that stops wiring any of the three booleans
  trips the test.

Drive-by: absorb rustfmt's preferred wrapping for three adjacent
\`serde_json::from_slice(...).expect(...)\` call sites in the
\`long_running_maintenance_story\` test so the file stays
rustfmt-clean.

Pure test strengthening. No production code touched.
